The key points consistently highlighted include their early onset around the 11th week of gestation compared to other paranasal sinuses such as the maxillary, frontal, and sphenoidal sinuses, which develop significantly later. These findings align with both historical references and more current anatomical studies emphasizing the unique timing of ethmoid sinus development. This selection is justified by the emphasis on the ethmoid sinuses' pioneering role in paranasal sinus development during fetal stages.
